budget_wines.name: Domane Wachau Terrassen Federspiel Gruner Veltliner
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available directly at Domane Wachau in Durnstein, at Wachau wine retailers, and via major Austrian online retailers including Wein und Co.
budget_wines.description: The Terrassen Federspiel Gruner Veltliner is Domane Wachau's entry-level Codex wine, sourced from grower-member terraced plots across the appellation. It shows the classic Gruner Veltliner profile of white pepper, green herbs, and fresh citrus at moderate alcohol, representing a reliable and widely available example of the Wachau at an accessible price.

budget_wines.name: Domane Wachau Terrassen Federspiel Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Domane Wachau cellar door in Durnstein, Austrian wine merchants, and online via Wein und Co and Hawesko.
budget_wines.description: The Terrassen Federspiel Riesling from Domane Wachau shows the clean stone-fruit and mineral character of Wachau Riesling at Federspiel level. Bright acidity and moderate body make it approachable young while retaining the regional terroir signature at a price point well under EUR 25.

budget_wines.name: Weingut Jamek Federspiel Gruner Veltliner
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Jamek in Joching, selected Vienna wine shops, and Austrian wine specialist retailers.
budget_wines.description: Weingut Jamek, one of the historic Vinea Wachau members based in Joching, produces a Federspiel Gruner Veltliner that captures the mineral and herbal character of their Danube-terraced plots at an accessible price. The Federspiel tier sits in the approachable range while still carrying the site character that makes Jamek's Smaragd wines highly regarded.

budget_wines.name: Prager Hinter der Burg Federspiel Gruner Veltliner
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Prager in Weissenkirchen, selected Austrian and international wine merchants, and specialist retailers in Austria and Germany.
budget_wines.description: Prager's Hinter der Burg is the entry-level Federspiel Gruner Veltliner from one of the Wachau's benchmark estates in Weissenkirchen. At under EUR 25, it provides a direct line to the Prager house style: precise, mineral-edged, with the regional white-pepper and herb character of Gruner Veltliner on a lighter Federspiel frame.

budget_wines.name: Hirtzberger Steinporz Federspiel Gruner Veltliner
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Hirtzberger in Spitz an der Donau, selected Austrian specialist retailers, and international importers including agents in the UK and US.
budget_wines.description: Hirtzberger's Steinporz is the accessible Federspiel-tier entry from a Spitz estate whose Smaragd Singerriedel Riesling commands serious collector attention. The Steinporz delivers the Hirtzberger terroir at a Federspiel weight: fresh, stony, and moderately structured, with the characteristic minerality of the gneiss and granite soils above Spitz.

budget_wines.name: Tegernseerhof Bergdistel Federspiel Gruner Veltliner
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Tegernseerhof in Durnstein, Vienna wine shops, and select Austrian export merchants.
budget_wines.description: Tegernseerhof's Bergdistel Federspiel Gruner Veltliner is a crisp, well-priced expression of Wachau Gruner Veltliner from one of the appellation's quietly consistent producers. Sourced from terraced plots in Durnstein, it shows the signature white-pepper and stone-fruit profile of Gruner Veltliner in the lighter Federspiel frame, ready to drink from release.

budget_wines.name: Weingut Schmelz Federspiel Gruner Veltliner
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Schmelz in Joching, local Wachau wine retailers, and via Austrian direct shipping.
budget_wines.description: Weingut Schmelz in Joching, a family estate with terraced plots above the Danube, produces a Federspiel Gruner Veltliner that is a local favourite for its clean, direct style. At an accessible price, it represents the straightforward pleasure of fresh Wachau Gruner Veltliner without the premium attached to single-vineyard Smaragd bottlings.

budget_wines.name: Domane Wachau Steinfeder Gruner Veltliner
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Domane Wachau cellar shop in Durnstein, supermarkets in Lower Austria and Vienna, and via Austrian online retailers.
budget_wines.description: The lightest tier in the Wachau Codex classification, the Steinfeder Gruner Veltliner from Domane Wachau is a delicate, low-alcohol dry white with subtle white-pepper and floral aromatics. At under EUR 15, it is the most accessible expression of authentic Wachau wine and widely available in Austrian retail and at the cooperative's cellar in Durnstein.

budget_wines.name: Weingut Rudi Pichler Federspiel Gruner Veltliner
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Rudi Pichler in Weissenkirchen, selected Austrian and international wine specialist retailers.
budget_wines.description: Weingut Rudi Pichler, distinct from F.X. Pichler and based in Weissenkirchen, produces a focused Federspiel Gruner Veltliner that is an approachable entry point to the estate's range. The Federspiel carries the mineral and herbal character of the Weissenkirchen hillside plots at a moderate price, making it a value option for visitors exploring the estate's Smaragd range on subsequent visits.

budget_wines.name: Domane Wachau Steinfeder Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Domane Wachau cellar shop in Durnstein and Austrian retail stockists including Wein und Co and Interspar.
budget_wines.description: At the lightest and most affordable end of the Wachau Riesling range, Domane Wachau's Steinfeder Riesling offers a clean, stone-fruit-driven dry white at a price accessible to first-time Wachau visitors. The low alcohol and bright acidity make it a versatile food wine and an easy introduction to the Steinfeder tier of the Codex Wachau classification.
